organization.name: Lone Star Hope Initiative
organization.description: Lone Star Hope Initiative (LSHI) is a non-profit organization established to promote the protection, rehabilitation, education, and psychosocial wellbeing of vulnerable street children and adolescents, with particular emphasis on the girl child and youth affected by substance use.

address.line1: No. 7 Oscar’s Place, New Victory Close, off Obirikwere Bridge by East-West Road

objectives.title: Psychological Rehabilitation
objectives.description: Provide psychological rehabilitation and basic healthcare services for abused street children and adolescents, especially the girl child.

objectives.title: Drug Rehabilitation.
objectives.description: Rehabilitate adolescents dependent on psychoactive substance use through professional counselling and structured recovery programs.

objectives.title: Family Reunion
objectives.description: Reunite street children with their families where safe and appropriate, restoring bonds and providing follow-up support.

objectives.title: Skills & Livelihood
objectives.description: Provide enabling environments for skills acquisition and livelihood development, empowering street children toward self-sufficiency.

objectives.title: Education Support
objectives.description: Provide scholarships and support for basic education, ensuring every child has access to quality learning regardless of circumstance.

objectives.title: Awareness Programs
objectives.description: Conduct extensive mental health and substance-use awareness programs in government primary and secondary schools across communities.

team.name: Dr Ugwu Nneka Valeria-Offiah
team.bio: Medical Doctor, Mental health Professional and Gospel music Minister with years of experience in mental health and public health awareness.

team.name: Lady Uchenna Bassey
team.bio: An Economist with years of experience in the humanitarian sector.

team.name: Dr David Iberedem Clinton
team.bio: A medical Doctor and Specialist Psychiatrist with years of experience in mental health and addiction psychiatry.

team.name: Dr Justin Ikor Ekpa
team.bio: Dr Justin Ikor Ekpa A medical Doctor and Information Technology expert with years of working experience in the Humanitarian sector.

team.name: Lady Nkechi Biddy Offiah
team.bio: A retired educationist , Guidance counsellor and civil servant with the Federal Ministry of Education in Nigeria, with several years of working experience with the humanitarian sector.
